6:35pm - The Owl & Co. Write a forum convo saying that from Friday the channel extends to 9pm
User1: Hey, did you hear about the new schedule change on CBBC?

User2: No, what’s happening?

User1: Starting from Friday, the channel will be extending its programming all the way to 9pm!

User2: No way, that’s awesome! More shows to watch in the evening, I can’t wait!

User1: Yeah, it’s going to be great. Can’t wait to see what they have in store for us with the extended schedule.

User2: Definitely, CBBC is really stepping up their game. I’m looking forward to tuning in for the evening shows.
Write a follow up with the new schedule (still fictional/part of the scenario) from 7am to 9pm starting April 25, Incidentally some of the shows only seen in the mornings close out the final hour

7:25am - Arthur
7:50am - Danger Mouse

User1: Have you seen the new CBBC schedule extending all the way to 9pm now?

User2: Yes, I’m so excited! It’s great to have even more shows to watch in the evenings.

User1: I know, I can’t believe some of the morning shows are now on in the evening too. It’s going to be a fun night of TV.

User2: Definitely, I’m looking forward to catching up on all my favorite shows. CBBC is really spoiling us with this extended schedule.
Write a follow up noting TO&C is nowhere to be seen
User1: Hey, have you noticed that The Owl & Co. isn’t on the new extended CBBC schedule?

User2: You’re right, I just looked through the entire lineup and it’s nowhere to be seen. I wonder why they decided to leave it out.

User1: It’s a shame, I really enjoyed that show. Hopefully, they bring it back or replace it with something equally as entertaining.

User2: Yeah, hopefully they have some new exciting shows lined up for the evening time slot. I’ll miss The Owl & Co. but I’m sure there will be plenty of other great shows to watch. Write a forum convo about what’s most popular on CBBC right now (in this scenario), what isn’t doing well and what’s basically surviving
User1: So what do you think is currently the most popular show on CBBC?

User2: I would have to say that The Next Step seems to be really popular at the moment. It has a big fan base and always gets a lot of views.

User1: Yeah, I agree. It’s definitely a hit with the viewers. But what about shows that aren’t doing so well?

User2: I think Remotely Funny hasn’t been getting as much attention as some of the other shows. It’s a fun show, but it just doesn’t seem to be catching on with the audience.

User1: And what about the shows that are just surviving on the schedule?

User2: I’d say Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed is one of those shows that’s just comfortably hanging in there. It’s not the most popular, but it has its loyal viewers who tune in regularly.

User1: It’s interesting to see how different shows are faring on the channel. I wonder if CBBC will make any changes based on the popularity of certain shows. Write a forum convo about the early morning only shows, and one of them is doing pretty good for a forgotten cartoon that only airs first thing
User1: Have you noticed how well Skunk Fu is doing as one of the early morning only shows on CBBC?

User2: Yes, it’s actually surprising how popular it has become considering it only airs first thing in the morning. It seems to have gained a new following among viewers.

User1: That’s great to hear! It’s always nice to see a forgotten cartoon do well and find a new audience. I hope CBBC continues to showcase these early morning gems. Write a Wikipedia page set in May 2016 with current programming (with the year it originally debuted on the channel, and if a show has left for a period, then the year it returned will appear next to it, by the way its still part of this scenario)
CBBC (Children’s BBC) is a British children’s television channel owned and operated by the BBC. As of May 2016, the channel offers a variety of popular programming for children of all ages.

Current programming on CBBC:

- Skunk Fu (2007-2010, January 02 2016-)
- Arthur (2002)
- Danger Mouse (2015)
- The Next Step (2013)
- Operation Ouch! (2015)
- Horrible Histories (2009)
- Blue Peter (1959)
- Odd Squad (2014)
- Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed
- Scream Street
- So Awkward
- Shaun the Sheep (2007)
- Class Dismissed
- Jamie Johnson
- Remotely Funny
- Lost and Found
- The Dumping Ground
- The Zoo
- The Playlist
- Now You See It
- Junior Vets
- Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
- Deadly Art
- The Deep (March 01 2016-)
- Hetty Feather
- Millie Inbetween

One of the standout shows that has become a surprise success is Skunk Fu, which originally debuted on the channel in 2007. Despite being a forgotten cartoon that only airs in the early morning, it has managed to attract a new audience and has become a popular show among viewers.

Overall, CBBC continues to offer a diverse range of programming that caters to the interests and preferences of its young audience. Viewers can expect entertaining and educational shows that keep them engaged and coming back for more. Write a schedule change on May 1 which sees some suprises in the evening lineup

Viewers can expect a mix of fan-favorite shows and new additions to the schedule for an entertaining and engaging evening of programming on CBBC.
Write a follow up where a user wonders when Owl is back, Also on bbc.com/cbbc we are surprised at the top cartoon on the Iplayer section
User1: Hey, do you know when The Owl & Co. will be back on CBBC? I miss watching that show.

User2: I’m not sure, but hopefully it will return soon. It was a fun show to watch in the mornings.

User1: Yeah, it really was. I’ll have to keep an eye out for any updates on when it might come back.

User2: In the meantime, have you checked out the CBBC section on the BBC website? There’s a surprise at the top cartoon in the iPlayer section.

Write a convo on March 14 2016 where the new logo begins, a viewer tunes in and says the DOG (on screen bug) looks too opaque and hopes they make it transparent soon "Also, Skunk Fu is back on? Since when? Wasn't that dropped a few years ago"

User1: Did you see the new CBBC logo that started today, March 14, 2016? They've changed the design, but I've got to say, the DOG (on-screen bug) looks quite opaque. It's a bit distracting.

User2: Yeah, I noticed that too. I hope they make it more transparent soon. It shouldn't overpower the shows.

User1: True. And, speaking of shows, have you noticed Skunk Fu is back on the schedule? Since when did that happen? I thought it was dropped a few years back.

User2: It’s a surprise for me too! Skunk Fu officially returned to the CBBC lineup as of January 02, 2016. I guess they’re bringing it back for the nostalgia factor, or maybe it’s gained a bit of a cult following.

User1: Wow, that's pretty cool! It’s always nice when they bring back shows from the past. I'll have to set an alarm to catch it in the mornings then. But first, let's hope they adjust that DOG. It's really catching too much of my attention away from the shows.
Write a convo in May where NYSI is going away

User1: Hey, did you hear the news about Now You See It (NYSI)? I'm kind of bummed out.

User2: No, what happened? Are they changing the schedule again?

User1: Worse. NYSI is going off the air. It won’t be part of the CBBC lineup anymore after this season wraps up.

User2: Seriously? That’s such a disappointment! I loved the magic tricks and all the amazing illusions. It was something different compared to the rest of the programming.

User1: Yeah, I know what you mean. It was a great mix of entertainment and learning. I guess all good things have to come to an end at some point.

User2: Have they given any reason for why it’s going away? Is there any chance it’ll come back?

User1: As far as I can tell, they haven’t given a specific reason. Could be due to ratings or maybe they’re making room for new shows. I hope it does come back though. It really was a gem in the schedule.

User2: That’s the thing with TV, always changing. I’ll definitely miss it. Do you know when the last episode is airing?

User1: Yeah, the final episode of this season is set for the end of this month. You might want to catch it live, make the most of it while you still can.

User2: Definitely going to do that. Thanks for letting me know. Maybe we should start a petition or something to bring it back. What do you think?

User1: That’s not a bad idea at all. If enough fans show their support, maybe CBBC will consider bringing it back in some form. Worth a try!
Write a convo wondering when the Owl & Co is coming back,

User1: Hey, have you heard anything about when The Owl & Co. might be making a comeback to CBBC?

User2: No news yet, I've been checking periodically because I really miss that show. It's like it just vanished with no explanation.

User1: Same here. It was such a unique and funny series. Do you think it might have something to do with scheduling or are they planning a revamp?

User2: Honestly, it's hard to tell. CBBC hasn't released any official statements about it. I'm hoping it's just a temporary hiatus and not a complete cancellation.

User1: That would be the best-case scenario. Maybe they're working on new episodes? The show was quite popular, after all.

User2: True, it did have a good fan base. I think reaching out to CBBC or starting some kind of petition might show them there's still a demand for The Owl & Co.

User1: That's a great idea! A petition could definitely get their attention. Plus, checking out fan forums and social media might give us some clues about any potential return.

User2: Absolutely, let's keep our ears to the ground and spread the word. The Owl & Co. deserves a spot back on the air.

User1: Agreed. For now, we wait and hope for some good news soon. Let's reconnect if either of us hears anything promising.

User2: Definitely. Fingers crossed we get to see our favorite owl and his adventures again soon!
Write a follow up where on 10 May there will be no TNS on the lineup for 1 day, There promoting this with the song "I Don't Feel Like Dancing", especially the line "no sir no dancing today"

User1: Have you seen the recent promo for CBBC’s schedule change on the 10th of May? It's quite clever, actually.

User2: No, I haven't caught it yet. What's happening on the 10th?

User1: They're not airing The Next Step (TNS) on that day, just for a break. And they're using the song "I Don't Feel Like Dancing" to promote this, especially highlighting the line "no sir, no dancing today."

User2: Oh, that's quite a thematic way to announce it! I love how they've tied the song into the fact that there'll be no TNS that day. But why the break?

User1: They haven’t given a specific reason, but it seems like it's just a one-day thing—maybe for a special event or different programming that day. It caught my attention though; very creative marketing!

User2: Definitely. It's refreshing to see a channel use humor and creativity like that. So, any idea what they are planning to air instead?

User1: They haven’t disclosed much about the alternative programming yet. But if they’re putting this much effort into the announcement, it’s probably something worth tuning in for.

User2: True that. It’s nice to have a little shake-up in the schedule now and then. Keeps things interesting. I just hope the TNS fans won't be too disappointed about the pause.

User1: Agreed. But with the playful way they’ve communicated it, I think they’ve softened the blow. Plus, it’s just for one day. Gives everyone something to anticipate when TNS returns.

User2: Well, I’m certainly curious to see what CBBC has planned for that day. And it’s good to know TNS will be back right after. Thanks for the heads up!

User1: No problem! Let’s keep an eye out for any more quirky promos. CBBC seems to be stepping up their game.
Write a follow up with the alternative schedule,

User1: Hey, I finally caught a glimpse of what CBBC is planning for the 10th of May when The Next Step takes a break. They’ve released the alternative schedule!

User2: Oh, really? I've been curious about what they had up their sleeves. What’s on the lineup?

User1: So, from what I've gathered, they're starting off the day with a special marathon of "Horrible Histories," which I think is a fantastic choice. Tons of episodes back-to-back.

User2: That sounds brilliant! "Horrible Histories" has always been a crowd-pleaser. What else?

User1: After the "Horrible Histories" marathon, they're airing a couple of exclusive episodes of "Operation Ouch!" which, if I heard right, will include some never-before-seen content.

User2: That’s amazing! "Operation Ouch!" is both educational and fun. Great for keeping the kids engaged. Any word on evening programming?

User1: Yes, the evening is going to be just as exciting. They’ve scheduled a "Blue Peter" special, promising some unique segments and guest appearances. Right after that, there's a movie slot – "Swallows and Amazons," a family adventure film.

User2: Wow, they're really pulling out all the stops! "Blue Peter" followed by a movie is a solid lineup. It sounds like CBBC is making the most out of the day without TNS.

User1: Absolutely, and they’re ending the day with a "Shaun the Sheep" marathon, which I think is the cherry on top. Perfect for winding down before bed.

User2: Couldn't agree more. This alternative schedule actually sounds like a refreshing change. Kudos to CBBC for mixing things up and keeping the content lively.

User1: Definitely. I'm almost glad we're getting a break from TNS now. This lineup has something for everyone. Can't wait to see how it all goes down on the 10th. 

User2: Me too. I'll be tuning in for sure. Let's circle back after and chat about our favorite segments from the day!
Write the new schedule, which runs from 7am to 9pm on 10 May

Given the scenario details and the conversation regarding the absence of "The Next Step" on May 10 and the planned special programming for that day, here’s how the new schedule could potentially look, running from 7am to 9pm:

**CBBC Special Schedule for May 10:**

**Morning Block:**

- 7:00am - "Shaun the Sheep" - Early riser special episodes to start the day with laughter.
- 7:30am - "Horrible Histories Marathon" - A mix of fan-favorite episodes and special segments, providing a humorous take on history until noon.

**Midday Block:**

- 12:00pm - "Operation Ouch! Special Episodes" - Offering never-before-seen content focusing on fascinating medical and scientific curiosities.
- 1:30pm - "Art Ninja" - Special episode filled with creative and artistic challenges and projects.
  
**Afternoon Block:**

- 2:00pm - "Blue Peter Special" - An extended episode with unique challenges, celebrity guest appearances, and engaging DIY crafts.
- 3:00pm - "Deadly 60" - A special episode featuring some of the most exciting adventures and encounters from the series.
- 3:30pm - "The Zoo" - Get up close with the funniest and most unexpected antics of animals in the zoo in these special compilations.

**Evening Block:**

- 4:00pm - "So Awkward" - Two back-to-back episodes of this teen comedy favorite.
- 5:00pm - "Swallows and Amazons" - Film airing. A family adventure story of sailing, exploration, and an exciting summer.
- 6:45pm - "Jamie Johnson" - A special episode of the football-themed drama series.
  
**Night Block:**

- 7:30pm - "Horrible Histories" - Another chance to catch up with today’s marathon highlights.
- 8:00pm - "My Life: A Newsround Special" - Documentary special following the lives of extraordinary kids.
- 8:30pm - "Shaun the Sheep" Marathon - Ending the day with heartwarming and hilarious episodes for a cozy night in.

Please note, the descriptions provided are for illustrative purposes to reflect the variety and entertainment value planned for the special day without "The Next Step."
Write a tweet noticing it's not just TNS gone today. And list some other shows gone today,  but they'll all be back tomorrow

🚨 Noticed something interesting on #CBBC's schedule today? It's not just The Next Step (#TNS) taking a brief hiatus; we're also missing our daily dose of #MillieInbetween, #DangerMouse, & #OddSquad! But don't worry, it's just for today. All your faves will be back in action tomorrow. Enjoy the special lineup! 📺✨ #CBBCSpecialDay
Write a follow up where a user partially has the May 11 schedule from 7am to 8:30am,

User1: Hey, everyone! Managed to catch a sneak peek at the schedule for May 11th from 7am to 8:30am after today's special programming. Looks like things are getting back to normal on #CBBC. 

User2: Oh? Do share! I’ve been missing my regular shows. What’s on the lineup for the early birds?

User1: No worries! It kicks off with #DangerMouse at 7:00am, sharp as ever. Then we’re back to back-to-back adventures with #OperationOuch at 7:15am, giving us our daily dose of fascinating facts and science.

User2: That’s more like it! What about #TheNextStep and the others?

User1: Right after, at 7:45am, #TheNextStep makes its return! So those who were missing the drama and dance can breathe easy. And to round off the early morning, #OddSquad is on at 8:00am, solving mysteries in their unique way.

User2: That sounds fantastic! It'll be good to have our routine back. Did they mention anything else?

User1: After that, #MillieInbetween is scheduled for 8:15am, ready to tackle the day with more family adventures. That's all I caught for now, but it's shaping up to be a return to the shows we love.

User2: Perfect, thanks for the update! Can't wait to tune in again. It was fun having a change, but there's something comforting about the familiar. #CBBC 📺🎉
Write a follow up wondering where SF is, saying it vanished after May 9, and asking when it will be back, and a user tells us when

User1: Hey #CBBC fans, has anyone else noticed that #SkunkFu vanished from the lineup after May 9th? I woke up expecting my morning dose of martial arts antics, and it was nowhere to be found. Any idea when it will be back?

User2: Yeah, I noticed that too! I thought I had just missed it, but it seems like it's actually off the schedule. 😢

User3: I was wondering the same! #SkunkFu added a fun kickstart to my mornings. Missing the laughs and lessons from the Valley already.

User4: Good news, folks! I caught a post from CBBC earlier. #SkunkFu is taking a short break but will be making its return to the morning block next Monday! They're reshuffling a few shows but assured that all our favorites, including Skunk Fu, will be back in action. Mark your calendars for May 16th! 📅🥋 

User1: That's a relief to hear! Thanks for sharing, @User4. I was worried it might have been dropped for good. Can't wait for its return. #SkunkFuFan

User2: Awesome, thanks for the update! Monday mornings will have their mojo back with #SkunkFu. Can't wait! 🌟

User3: Fantastic news! The Valley awaits our return. Thanks for clearing that up, @User4! #SkunkFuComeback

User4: No problem, happy to help spread the good news! Let's gear up for next week. #CBBC #SkunkFu 📺
Write a follow up asking which timeslot

User1: Great to hear #SkunkFu is coming back on May 16th! Does anyone know which timeslot it will be returning to? I want to make sure I don't miss it. My mornings haven’t been the same without it!

User4: Just checked the latest update from CBBC for you! #SkunkFu will be reclaiming its original morning timeslot at 7:00am. Perfect timing to kickstart the day with some martial arts wisdom and humor!

User1: Perfect, 7:00am it is! Thanks a bunch, @User4! My alarm is set. Can't wait to see what adventures await in the Valley.

User2: Back to 7:00am? Awesome news! Looks like I’ve got a reason to wake up early again. Thanks for the info! 

User3: 7:00am slot is classic #SkunkFu. Happy days! Thanks for confirming, @User4. Really appreciate it.
